articles.title: STD::MOVE DOESN'T MOVE ANYTHING: A DEEP DIVE INTO VALUE CATEGORIES
articles.summary: `std::move` in C++ is a misnamed function that doesn't physically move data, but rather casts an expression into an rvalue reference, signaling that its resources can be "pillaged." This conversion allows move constructors and move assignment operators to efficiently transfer ownership of resources, usually by swapping pointers, instead of performing expensive deep copies.

articles.title: WHAT HAPPENED TO WEBASSEMBLY
articles.summary: WebAssembly today is used in many real-world applications, powering everything from game engines and image processing to secure plugin ecosystems. It functions as an efficient, low-level compilation target, allowing devs to bridge language gaps, integrate high-performance code (like C++) into web environments, and run many languages efficiently.

articles.title: AI IS A BUSINESS MODEL STRESS TEST
articles.summary: AI is a business model stress test. Tailwind Labs was recently tested as it laid off 75% of its engineering team because AI models reduced traffic to its documentation, reducing sales. AI commoditizes anything "specifiable," such as documentation or pre-built components, by directly generating answers without referring users to original sources.

articles.title: START YOUR MEETINGS AT 5 MINUTES PAST
articles.summary: Starting all meetings five minutes past the hour or half-hour guarantees a short break between back-to-back meetings, which is more effective than attempting to end early. The brief pause allows attendees to transition, settle, and arrive less stressed.

articles.title: DON'T FALL INTO THE ANTI-AI HYPE
articles.summary: AI is permanently changing programming, making manual coding obsolete for many tasks. LLMs are able to complete complex projects in hours instead of weeks, showing their current power in areas like library modification, bug fixing, and code generation. AI is a continuation of code democratization, and devs need to embrace and adapt to these new tools to multiply their capabilities.

articles.title: FERRITE
articles.summary: Ferrite is a fast, lightweight text editor built with Rust and egui. It has WYSIWYG Markdown editing, tree viewers for structured data, syntax highlighting, various view modes including split view and Zen mode, and workspace capabilities.

articles.title: CLAUDE SWITCHER
articles.summary: The Claude Code Switcher automates scripts using Claude Code. It enables executable markdown files with shebang support, allowing AI prompts to function as standard Unix-style scripts that can pipe data and chain together.

articles.title: HOW MARKDOWN TOOK OVER THE WORLD
articles.summary: Markdown is a simple, plain text formatting system created by John Gruber in 2004 to make writing for the web easier than using complex HTML. It quickly became a fundamental tool used across nearly every facet of the modern tech world. Despite its adoption and the immense value it enables, Gruber freely shared Markdown, never seeking to monetize his invention.

articles.title: MAJOR SOFTWARE INCUMBENTS RESTRICT API ACCESS TO DEFEND AGAINST AI-DRIVEN COMPETITION
articles.summary: Salesforce, Datadog, and Epic are restricting API access to protect market share as AI speeds up development. Slack now limits third-party calls to one per minute.

articles.title: THE SOFTWARE CAMBRIAN EXPLOSION
articles.summary: A "software Cambrian explosion" will massively increase the amount of software created, making skilled engineers necessary for managing, scaling, and securing this new software.

articles.title: CODE IS CLAY
articles.summary: AI's automation of commodity programming will allow software engineers to focus on creative, "hypercube" ideas, transforming the field into a more valued craft like post-industrial pottery.

articles.title: CODE IS CHEAP NOW. SOFTWARE ISN'T
articles.summary: While AI tools have made code generation cheap and enabled a wave of disposable personal software, creating robust, maintainable, and impactful software still requires human engineering expertise, architectural judgment, and strategic problem-solving beyond just writing code.

articles.title: AI ZEALOTRY
articles.summary: AI development is a transformative and fun evolution, similar to the advent of compilers, as it automates mundane tasks and allows devs to focus on strategic thinking.
